system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

    From function virtual Foam::IOobject Foam::fileOperation::findInstance(const Foam::IOobject&, Foam::scalar, const Foam::word&) const
    in file global/fileOperations/fileOperation/fileOperation.C at line 869.

FOAM exiting'}, {'file': 'log.blockMesh', 'error_content': "ERROR: 
wrong token type - expected Scalar, found on line 14 the punctuation token ','

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_E2_E2models/runs/squareBend/3/system/blockMeshDict/vertices at line 14.

    From function Foam::Istream& Foam::operator>>(Istream&, doubleScalar&)
    in file lnInclude/Scalar.C at line 101.

FOAM exiting"}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Current Errors

Two errors persist from all previous attempts:

1. **`log.blockMesh`**: "wrong token type - expected Scalar, found on line 14 the punctuation token ','" — this is a malformed `blockMeshDict` syntax issue.
2. **`log.rhoSimpleFoam`**: Cannot find mesh files (cascading from blockMesh failure).

## Diagnosis of the Syntax Error

The error occurs at **line 14 of `vertices`** in `blockMeshDict`. Looking at the current `blockMeshDict`, line 14 contains a comma in a context where OpenFOAM expects a scalar value. This typically happens when the vertex list format is incorrect.

The issue is subtle: the vertices are listed with inline comments using `//`, and some versions of OpenFOAM (especially older ones or certain distributions) may have trouble parsing comments mixed directly into the vertex coordinate lists. Additionally, looking at the history, the vertex numbering and block topology have been the recurring source of problems.

## Different Approach: Clean, Minimal, Validated Configuration

Based on the history of 5 failed attempts with various block topologies, I recommend a **complete rewrite of `blockMeshDict`** with a clean, minimal, and syntactically unambiguous format that has been proven to work in the squareBend tutorial context.

### Proposed `blockMeshDict` Fix

Rewrite the entire `blockMeshDict` with explicit spacing and no inline comments in the vertex list:

```
vertices
(
    (-50,  25,  25)   // 0
    (-50,  25, -25)   // 1
    (-50, -25, -25)   // 2
    (-50, -25,  25)   // 3

    (-500,  25,  25)  // 4
    (-500,  25, -25)  // 5
    (-500, -25, -25)  // 6
    (-500, -25,  25)  // 7
);

blocks
(
    hex (0 1 5 4 3 2 6 7) (50 50 50) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 2 1)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (4 7 6 5)
        );
    }

    upperWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 5 4)
            (1 4 7 5)
        );
    }

    lowerWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (3 2 6 7)
            (2 3 7 6)
        );
    }

    leftWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 7 4)
        );
    }

    rightWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (1 2 6 5)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);
```

### Key Fixes

1. **Removed inline comments from vertex coordinates**: While `//` comments are generally supported, removing them eliminates any potential parsing ambiguity that could trigger the "expected Scalar" error at line 14.
2. **Kept the single-block topology** with `hex (0 1 5 4 3 2 6 7)` — this was the only block ordering that passed in Attempt 1 before subsequent changes broke it.
3. **Maintained all boundary patches** to match the user requirement (inlet, outlet, upperWall, lowerWall, leftWall, rightWall).
4. **Clean, consistent formatting** with proper parentheses and spacing.

## Additional Issue: `alphat` Boundary Condition Mismatch

The `alphat` file references `Default_Boundary_Region` which doesn't exist in this mesh. Update `0/alphat` to replace the `Default_Boundary_Region` entry with individual entries for each boundary patch, or simplify to:

```
boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            calculated;
        value           uniform 0;
    }
    outlet
    {
        type            calculated;
        value           uniform 0;
    }
    upperWall
    {
        type            compressible::alphatWallFunction;
        Prt             0.85;
        value           uniform 0;
    }
    lowerWall
    {
        type            compressible::alphatWallFunction;
        Prt             0.85;
        value           uniform 0;
    }
    leftWall
    {
        type            compressible::alphatWallFunction;
        Prt             0.85;
        value           uniform 0;
    }
    rightWall
    {
        type            compressible::alphatWallFunction;
        Prt             0.85;
        value           uniform 0;
    }
}
```

## Execution Order

1. Fix `system/blockMeshDict` with the cleaned-up format above.
2. Run `blockMesh` — this should succeed without syntax errors.
3. Then run `rhoSimpleFoam`.

The root cause across all 5 attempts has been the block topology and/or syntax parsing issues. This approach isolates the problem to a known-working single-block configuration with clean syntax.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Replace the block definition from hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) to hex (0 1 5 4 3 2 6 7) to fix non-manifold topology; Remove inline comments from vertex coordinates to avoid parsing issues; Replace upperWall face (1 4 6 2) with (1 4 7 5) to match correct block vertex order; Add missing leftWall face (0 3 7 4); Add missing rightWall face (1 2 6 5); Keep all other boundary patches unchanged'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1000. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 1.0.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
